description: Populate solverState for the initial Big-M artificial-root spanning tree: supply vector, artificial arcs, initial basis, and initial potentials.
commit: feat: build the initial Big-M spanning tree and potentials
steps: Create init.go in the repo root (SPDX BSL-1.0, package mcf).

Implement `func buildInitialState(arcs []Arc, n, source, sink int, demand *uint256.Int) *solverState`. Assume inputs already validated. Set mReal = len(arcs); call newSolverState(n, mReal); set s.n = n, s.mReal = mReal, s.root = n.

Populate real-arc columns [0, mReal): s.source[i] = arcs[i].From, s.target[i] = arcs[i].To, s.cost[i] = arcs[i].Cost, s.capacity[i] = arcs[i].Capacity (alias, do not copy), and s.state[i] = stateLower (all real arcs start at the lower bound with flow 0).

Build the supply vector on solverState (int64, best-effort cost tracking only does not need supply; supply drives artificial arc orientation). Use a local `supply []int64` slice of length n+1 where `supply[source] = +1`, `supply[sink] = -1`, others 0. Note: the *magnitude* of supply for artificial arc flow uses demand (uint256). Supply sign drives only arc direction in the artificial tree.

Set M := bigM(n). For each real node v in [0, n), populate artificial arc index artIdx = mReal + v: if supply[v] >= 0 set s.source[artIdx] = v, s.target[artIdx] = s.root, dirNode[v] = dirUp; else set s.source[artIdx] = s.root, s.target[artIdx] = v, dirNode[v] = dirDown. Set s.cost[artIdx] = M; s.state[artIdx] = stateTree; s.capacity[artIdx] is set to a fresh uint256 equal to demand (call `new(uint256.Int).Set(demand)`); s.flow[artIdx] is set to demand for nodes whose |supply|==1 (source and sink) and to zero otherwise (zero already, from newSolverState).

Initialize the initial basis linkage: s.parent[v] = s.root for every v in [0,n); s.parent[s.root] = -1; s.predArc[v] = mReal + v; s.predArc[s.root] = -1; s.thread forms a linear thread visiting root, 0, 1, ..., n-1 and back (s.thread[root] = 0, s.thread[i] = i+1 for i in [0,n-1), s.thread[n-1] = root); set s.revThread to be the inverse of thread; s.succNum[v] = 1 for leaves; s.succNum[s.root] = n+1; s.lastSucc[v] = v for leaves; s.lastSucc[s.root] = n-1. Document inline that this mirrors LEMON's `init()` exactly.

Set initial potentials: s.pi[s.root] = 0; for v in [0,n): if dirNode[v] == dirUp then s.pi[v] = -M else s.pi[v] = +M. Comment that this makes every artificial arc's reduced cost 0 (they are in the basis).
Initialize pricing cursor state: s.nextArc = 0; s.blockSize = at-least-one and equal to `max(1, int(math.Ceil(math.Sqrt(float64(mReal)))))` unless mReal == 0 in which case blockSize = 1.

Create init_test.go. TestBuildInitialStateSmallGraph constructs a 3-node graph with source=0, sink=2, demand=5, and arcs (0->1 cost=1 cap=10), (1->2 cost=1 cap=10). Call buildInitialState and assert: s.mReal==2, s.m==5, s.root==3; every real arc has state==stateLower and flow zero; artificial arc source (index 2, since mReal+0) has flow equal to 5 and cost == bigM(3); s.parent[v]==s.root for v in [0,3); every artificial arc has state==stateTree; every real arc's reducedCost returns an int64 (just invoke to prove no panic); s.pi[s.root] == 0 and |s.pi[v]| == bigM(3) for v in [0,3). TestBuildInitialStateBlockSize asserts blockSize == max(1, ceil(sqrt(mReal))) for mReal in {1, 4, 7, 16, 100}.
acceptance_criteria: buildInitialState returns a *solverState with the documented artificial-root tree and initial potentials.
Every artificial arc has cost == bigM(n) and state == stateTree.
Every real arc has state == stateLower and flow == 0 after construction.
Source and sink artificial arcs carry flow equal to demand; other artificial arcs carry flow 0.
blockSize is at least 1 and equals ceil(sqrt(mReal)) for mReal >= 1.
